_docs: SENTINEL MCP configuration examples. Copy the block that matches your setup into your editor's MCP config. All examples use stdio transport.

local_development._description: Run from the project root using uv (recommended for contributors). No cwd needed when opening the project in your editor.

global_install._description: After `curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash` or `irm .../install.ps1 | iex`. Replace ~/.sentinel with your SENTINEL_HOME if customized.

global_entry_point._description: After `pip install sentinel` or `uv tool install sentinel`, sentinel-mcp is on PATH.

uvx_from_github._description: Run directly from GitHub — no local clone required. Useful for CI/CD agents and ephemeral environments.

with_claude_enrichment._description: Enable Claude LLM enrichment. Top findings get AI-generated attack narratives and remediation guidance.

custom_rules_and_plugins._description: Load extra rule directories and plugin directories at startup.

cline_vscode._description: Cline (VS Code extension). Add under Settings > Cline > MCP Servers, or paste into cline_mcp_settings.json.

continue_vscode._description: Continue (VS Code / JetBrains extension). Add to ~/.continue/config.json under mcpServers.

cursor_windows._description: Windows absolute path example for Cursor / Windsurf when not using uv globally.

ci_github_actions._description: CI/CD: run sentinel_diff as a security gate in GitHub Actions. Use uvx for zero-install execution.
ci_github_actions._usage: In your workflow: uvx --from git+https://github.com/Wembie/Sentinel sentinel audit ./  --format sarif --output sentinel.sarif
